# context-radar

> A catalogue rating GitHub projects on agentic context efficiency: how cheaply an AI
> coding agent can build the context it needs to work on a project, measured in tokens.
> Each project is scored 0 to 5 on seven dimensions; the weighted total is a radar
> score out of 100, mapped to a grade (A to F) and an adoption recommendation
> (adopt, trial, assess, hold).

## Scoring dimensions

- Agent instructions (weight 20)
- Code navigability and indexing (weight 20)
- Documentation structure (weight 15)
- Retrieval affordances (weight 15)
- Repository signal-to-noise (weight 10)
- Verification loops (weight 10)
- Context budget footprint (weight 10)

radar_score = sum over dimensions of (weight * score / 5). Maximum 100.

## Docs

- Methodology: methodology.md
- Glossary: glossary.md
- Catalogue index: projects/index.md

## Catalogue

Each entry is a Markdown file whose YAML frontmatter is the structured record,
validated against schema/project-analysis.schema.json.

- tokensave (mcp-server): radar 82, grade B, recommendation trial. projects/example-tokensave.md

## Notes

These ratings are decision support. A human should make and document any adoption
decision that materially affects a team or product.
